message:
test(progress): make progress-bar tests actually assert on observable state

The four tests in `src/util/progress::tests` previously only exercised
the code path ("should not panic or produce output") without asserting
any behavior. That gave them zero signal — they passed even when the
underlying indicatif bar was broken, and a recent audit (bead p7xo /
mock-code-finder scan) flagged them as tests-without-assertions.

Replace the passive "did it not panic" assumption with explicit
assertions on the ProgressBar / ProgressTracker state at each step:

- `test_progress_bar_hidden_when_not_terminal` now asserts
  `length() == Some(100)` at construction, `position() == 0` before
  increment, `position() == 50` after `inc(50)`, and `is_finished()`
  after `finish()`.
- `test_spinner_hidden_when_not_terminal` asserts `length() == None`
  (spinners have no length), initial position zero, and
  `is_finished()` after `finish()`.
- `test_progress_tracker_determinate` asserts the wrapped bar length,
  starting position, `is_showing()` matches `should_show_progress()`,
  the position bumps to 10 after the loop, `is_finished()` after
  `finish_with_message`, and the final message reads "Done".
- `test_progress_tracker_spinner` asserts `length() == None`,
  `is_showing()` matches the showing predicate, and — crucially —
  branches the expected initial message on whether the spinner is
  actually shown: `"Loading..."` if visible, `""` if hidden (because
  indicatif suppresses message state on hidden bars). Subsequent
  `set_message("Still loading...")` is asserted to update the
  message, and `finish_and_clear()` is asserted to mark the bar
  finished.
- `test_multi_progress_hidden` asserts length, starting position,
  position after `inc(5)`, and `is_finished()` after `finish()`.

No production code changed; these are strictly tests that now actually
test something.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
